assets.name: Touched, Feline
assets.summary: Feline-touched: night hunter's senses, impossible leaps, and predator stealth.
assets.postamble: Your touched value determines how many abilities you may use: Pure — none; Prime — 1; Second — 2; Third or Feral — all 3.

abilities.text: You move with the senses and poise of a night hunter. When you Face Danger, Secure an Advantage, or Gather Information in darkness, dim light, or heavy shadow, add +1. On a strong hit, also take +1 momentum as your night sight and feline awareness reveal more than others can see.

Your leaps and landings defy human limits. When you Face Danger or Undertake a Journey by jumping, climbing, or risking a fall, add +1 and roll +edge (even if another stat would normally apply). On a strong hit, you land sure-footed; take +1 momentum or inflict +1 harm if your leap carries you into the attack.

You vanish into stillness like a hidden hunter. When you remain motionless or stalk slowly in cover and then Secure an Advantage or Face Danger by relying on concealment, add +1. On a hit, you are not detected and take +1 momentum; on a strong hit, you may also improve your position (define a safe escape route, observe unnoticed, or set up an ally’s next move).

assets.name: Conclave Ritual: Cantrip
assets.summary: Minor magical tricks: clean, light, warm, lock, and more.
assets.description:
You have learned minor magical tricks — small, reliable effects drawn from mana seeds. Choose 2 cantrips when you acquire this asset.

Limitations: A cantrip cannot affect anything larger than you can hold in two hands, cannot last longer than 10 minutes or until dispelled (see level exceptions below), cannot reach farther than 10 paces (line of sight required), and cannot directly inflict harm in combat. Most cantrips take several seconds to cast, up to a few minutes for larger or more complex work.

cantrips.name: Tidy/Clean
cantrips.desc: Clean or create a minor mess on a surface.

cantrips.name: Mark/Unmark
cantrips.desc: Place or remove a small symbol or mark.

cantrips.name: Mute/Amplify
cantrips.desc: Mute or amplify a small sound.

cantrips.name: Freshen/Spoil
cantrips.desc: Freshen or spoil a small quantity of food/drink.

cantrips.name: Warm/Chill
cantrips.desc: Warm or cool a small object or liquid.

cantrips.name: Spark/Snuff
cantrips.desc: Light or extinguish a small, non-magical flame.

cantrips.name: Lock/Unlock
cantrips.desc: Jam or unjam a simple latch or buckle.

cantrips.name: Tint/Clear
cantrips.desc: Change the color of a small item or clear a small quantity of liquid.

cantrips.name: Stick/Unstick
cantrips.desc: Adhere or separate two small objects.

cantrips.name: Scout's Light
cantrips.desc: Produce a faint, firefly-like, temporary glow.

cantrips.name: Soften/Harden
cantrips.desc: Soften (make flexible) or harden (make brittle) a small spot or item.

abilities.text: When you perform a cantrip, Cast Conclave Ritual with a base difficulty of 1. Add +1 if cast at range, and +1 if cast under stress (e.g. in combat or while being watched). On a strong hit, the cantrip works as intended; take +1 momentum. On a weak hit, the cantrip works but choose one: it is weaker or shorter than intended, it draws unwanted attention, or you suffer -1 spirit from the strain. On a miss, the cantrip misfires; Pay the Price.
Choose 2 additional cantrips from the list (4 total). Your cantrips now last up to 1 hour or until you dismiss them.
Choose 2 additional cantrips from the list (6 total). Simple/touching cantrips (1 mana cost) are now free — they cost 0 mana.

assets.name: Conclave Ritual: Arcane Inspection
assets.summary: Sense and interpret mana in a person, object, or area.
assets.description: A basic ritual taught early to Neophytes and Disciples of the Conclave, allowing them to sense and interpret mana in a person, object, or area — "detect magic," grounded in the color-coded manite system. It uses primarily Amber to read patterns, with Luminous to stabilize and Gray as a structural aid when needed.

inspectionFactors.name: Presence & Purity
levels: Simple ping — only learn whether any mana is present (yes/no).
Presence + rough purity — know if mana is stable, mixed, or corrupted.
Detailed purity — distinguish refined Conclave-grade, wild blight, or Black/corrupted signatures.

inspectionFactors.name: Color Resolution
levels: No color discrimination — sense only mana present vs. none.
Broad categories — structural (Gray), vital (Azure/Verdant), energetic (Crimson/Luminous), mental/decay (Amber/Black).
Exact colors and proportions (e.g., mostly Gray with traces of Luminous and faint Amber residue).

inspectionFactors.name: Intensity & Scale
levels: Crude strength sense — gut impression of weak/moderate/strong.
Rough magnitude and footprint — estimate radius/volume (localized charm, room-wide ward, residual field).
Detailed mapping — locate hotspots, weak points, anchor nodes, and strongest direction of effect.

inspectionFactors.name: Temporal Insight
levels: No time sense — only perceive the current state.
Recent vs. old — tell if the effect is fresh, fading, or ancient, and whether it has been changed recently.
Event echoes — catch impressions of one or two key events: a recent casting, strong emotional imprint, or violent discharge.

inspectionFactors.name: Structural / Intent Insight
levels: Broad function only — sense whether the effect is protective, harmful, sensory, healing, or corruptive.
Simple structure — identify seed-based effect, ritual pattern, crude infusion, or natural blight; educated guess at intended use.
Detailed structure — infer likely seed shapes, probable triggering conditions, and one or two concrete capabilities or limitations.

inspectionFactors.name: Penetration & Subtlety
levels: Superficial scan — easily blocked by wards or barriers; casting is unobtrusive (faint prickle at most).
Moderate penetration — read through basic concealment or light Luminous masking; sensitive targets may feel they are being looked at.
Deep probe — push through sophisticated wards or read mana in living bodies or sealed containers; obvious to other casters, may trigger alarms or backlash.

inspectionExample.scenario: A Disciple wants to quietly check if a street-side shrine has been marked or trapped by magic, without lighting it up like a beacon.

presence.reason: Is there mana, and is it stable or corrupt?

penetration.reason: Peek through basic masking, no hard push.

inspectionExample.resolution: She commits 3 mana and Casts Conclave Ritual. She rolls 1d6[4] + 3 mana = 7 vs difficulty 5 and 1d10[9]. That is a weak hit — downgrade 2 factors (9 − 7 = 2). She drops Penetration from 1 to 0 (losing the ability to see through masking) and Color from 1 to 0 (only learning whether mana is present, not its type). She still senses a stable mana presence, gets a rough footprint, and knows the shrine’s effect is protective — enough to decide it’s safe.

abilities.text: You can perform an Arcane Inspection to sense and interpret mana in a person, object, or area. Record the total difficulty by summing the difficulty factors and Cast Conclave Ritual.
As above, but the spell difficulty is reduced by 2 (minimum 0) given mastery of the inspection skill.
As above, but the spell difficulty is reduced by 4 instead of 2 (minimum 0).

assets.name: Conclave Ritual: Illusion
assets.summary: Create visual, auditory, and physical illusions to deceive.
assets.description: You can cast illusion spells to deceive others. In combat, it is usually a ranged defensive style designed to confuse or distract the enemy. Illusions can also be cast outside of combat to create and hide traps and other items. Illusions can have visual, auditory, and physical components. Examples of illusions might be a small glamor, a veil of invisibility and silence around you while moving, phantom sounds, mirror image, hallucinations, altering or hiding terrain, or a ghostly touch.

inspectionFactors.name: Components
levels: One component: visual, auditory, or physical (touchable).
Two components.
All three components: visual, auditory, and physical.

inspectionFactors.name: Viewing
levels: Limited — convincing only from straight on.
Half sphere — convincing from most angles.
Full sphere — convincing from all angles, top and bottom.

inspectionFactors.name: Animation
levels: Static — no movement.
Small animated details — subtle motion. Requires red mana.
Full animation — lifelike movement. Requires red mana.

inspectionFactors.name: Persistence
levels: Only while actively casting.
Less than an hour. Requires red mana.
Up to two hours. Requires red mana.

inspectionFactors.name: Size
levels: Small — deck of cards.
Medium — chest sized.
Large — horse sized.

inspectionFactors.name: Detail
levels: Rough — obviously an illusion up close.
Passes casual inspection.
Lifelike — indistinguishable from real.

inspectionExample.scenario: You desire to distract a guard by creating a small hopping frog illusion.

viewing.reason: Half sphere — convincing from most angles.

size.reason: Small — deck of cards sized frog.

inspectionExample.resolution: You commit 4 mana and Cast Conclave Ritual. You roll 1d6[3] + 4 mana = 7 vs difficulty 5 and 1d10[8]. That is a weak hit — downgrade 1 factor (8 − 7 = 1). You drop Detail from 1 to 0 (rough — obviously an illusion up close), hoping the guard focuses on the movement and sound rather than inspecting the frog closely.

abilities.text: You can cast illusion spells to deceive others. Choose what you want to create, record the total difficulty by summing the difficulty factors, and Cast Conclave Ritual.
As above, but the spell difficulty is reduced by 2 (minimum 0) given mastery of the illusionist skill.
As above, but the spell difficulty is reduced by 4 instead of 2 (minimum 0).

assets.name: Conclave Ritual: Compulsion
assets.summary: Influence thoughts, emotions, and perceptions in a target's mind.
assets.description:
This ritual lets a caster influence thoughts, emotions, and perceptions in a target’s mind. It is more dangerous than simple illusions, because it acts inside the mind rather than in the air. Amber mana interfaces tightly with the nervous system and sensory processing. It can project illusions, dampen fear or pain, or induce specific emotional states; extended or forceful use risks addiction, mindbreak, or erosion of identity.

Risks: Repeated use on the same mind within a short period increases Resistance by +1 each time (max 2) and may create fragmented or contradictory memories. If you roll a miss on a compulsion with Depth 2 or Scope 2, mark a debility related to mental strain (sleeplessness, intrusive memories, emotional numbness). Unsanctioned deep control, if discovered by the Conclave, carries severe consequences.

inspectionFactors.name: Depth
levels: Surface nudge — momentary distraction, fleeting doubt, slight calm.
Emotional steering — fear, trust, guilt, confidence for minutes.
Directive compulsion — you plant a specific urge or instruction.

inspectionFactors.name: Clarity
levels: Vague impression — a feeling they can second-guess.
Convincing emotion or thought — they think it is their own.
Overriding priority — it dominates other concerns while it lasts.

inspectionFactors.name: Scope
levels: Single emotional axis — fear, calm, curiosity, etc.
Emotion plus a simple belief (“you can trust me,” “they are watching”).
Emotion plus a specific, framed memory or interpretation.

inspectionFactors.name: Duration
levels: Seconds — enough to slip a question past their guard.
Minutes.
Up to an hour or until jolted (pain, shock, another caster).

inspectionFactors.name: Resistance
levels: Willing / unsuspecting — they want to talk, or think you are helping.
Guarded / anxious — afraid, suspicious, but not trained.
Hardened — Brotherhood-trained, fanatic, or currently terrified.

inspectionFactors.name: Subtlety
levels: Crude push — they feel pushed, may notice something is off.
Gentle guidance — they rationalize it as their own reaction.
Seamless masking — memories fill in naturally; they are sure it was all their idea.

inspectionExample.scenario: You want to gently coax information from a frightened but untrained witness for a few minutes, nudging their fear away and trust toward you.

depth.reason: Emotional steering — calm them and build trust.

clarity.reason: Convincing — they think it is their own feeling.

scope.reason: Emotion + simple belief: “you can trust me.”

duration.reason: Minutes — long enough for a conversation.

inspectionExample.resolution: You commit 5 mana and Cast Conclave Ritual. You roll 1d6[3] + 5 mana = 8 vs difficulty 6 and 1d10[9]. That is a weak hit — downgrade 1 factor (9 − 8 = 1). You drop Subtlety from 1 to 0 (crude push). The witness calms down and talks freely, but afterward feels uneasy — something about the conversation felt off, and they may later question why they were so open with a stranger.

abilities.text: You can cast compulsion spells to influence thoughts, emotions, and perceptions. Decide the effect you want, record the total difficulty by summing the difficulty factors, and Cast Conclave Ritual. You must spend at least 1 mana.
As above, but the spell difficulty is reduced by 2 (minimum 0) given mastery of the compulsion skill.
As above, but the spell difficulty is reduced by 4 instead of 2 (minimum 0).

assets.name: Venenigisto
assets.summary: Poison crafter: brew contact and ingestion toxins from plant extracts, and detect the venomous work of others.
assets.preamble: If you have trained in the lore of plants, poisons, and their antidotes — whether as a herbalist, assassin, or apothecary.

abilities.text: When you craft a poison from foraged plants or gathered extracts, choose a potency and a delivery method. * Potency: soporific (induces sleep), debilitating (sickens and weakens), or lethal (kills if untreated). * Delivery: contact (applied to skin or a wound) or ingestion (slipped into food or drink). Suffer -1 supply and roll +wits. On a strong hit, you create a single dose; take +1 momentum. On a weak hit, you create it but choose one: suffer an additional -1 supply, the potency is weaker than intended, or the poison has a telltale sign (odor, taste, or color).

When you examine food, drink, a coated weapon, or a victim's symptoms to detect or identify a poison, add +1 when you Gather Information or Secure an Advantage (+wits). On a hit, you identify the poison and its effects. On a strong hit, you can also prepare an antidote if you have at least +1 supply; suffer -1 supply and the victim may Heal with +1 added.

When you craft a poison, add +1 and you may create two doses instead of one. Additionally, your poisons are subtle and hard to detect: when someone examines your poison, they must score a strong hit to identify it.

assets.name: Falsinto
assets.summary: Document forger: mimic handwriting, replicate seals and official papers, and detect the fakes of others.

abilities.text: You can identify paper types, ink composition, and handwriting characteristics. When you forge a person's handwriting: a letter, note, or simple written message: suffer -1 supply and roll +shadow. On a strong hit, the forgery is convincing and will withstand casual inspection; take +1 momentum. On a weak hit, you create it but choose one: suffer an additional -1 supply for better materials, the handwriting has a subtle inconsistency that someone familiar with the original may notice, or it takes much longer than expected.

When you examine a document for signs of forgery or tampering: inspecting paper, ink, seals, or handwriting: add +1 when you Gather Information or Secure an Advantage (+wits). On a hit, you determine its authenticity. On a strong hit, you also discern the forger's methods and habits; take +1 momentum.

You can now forge official documents, wax seals, stamped credentials, and other bureaucratic or institutional papers. When you do, use the same roll as above but the forgery may also include seals, signatures, and formal formatting. On a strong hit, the document passes even official scrutiny; take +1 momentum.
